nameMeanings.name: Gernet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: derivative of Middle High German gern ‘to desire’, a component of various ancient Germanic personal names such as Gernhard. Alternatively, a variant of Gernot .

variants.name: Gernot
meanings: German: from an ancient Germanic personal name composed of the elements gēr ‘spear’ + knōt- ‘(to) swing’.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Joan Gernet was born in Lythe, Yorkshire, England as the daughter of Benet Gernet and Joan. She had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Sir William Dacre. She died on 10 December 1361, in Eccleston, Lancashire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Joan Gernet est née à Lythe, Everwyck, Angleterre, fille de Benet Gernet et Joan. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Sir William Dacre. Elle est décédée le 10 décembre 1361, à Eccleston, Lancashire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Joan Gernet wurde in Lythe, Yorkshire, England, als Tochter von Benet Gernet und Joan geboren. Sie hatte mit Sir William Dacre mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 10. Dezember 1361 in Eccleston, Lancashire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Joan Gernet è nata il nel Lythe, Yorkshire, England come figlia di Benet Gernet e Joan. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia con Sir William Dacre. Morì il 10 dicembre 1361, nel Eccleston, Lancashire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan GernetはイギリスヨークシュアイアリスでBenet GernetとJoanの娘として生まれた。彼女はSir William Dacreとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1361年12月10日にイギリスランカシュアイアエククレストンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Gernet(은)는 영국 요르크실르 리테에서 Benet Gernet(와)과 Joan의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Sir William Dacre(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1361년 12월 10일 영국 랭카실르 치레스턴에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Joan Gernet nasceu em Lythe, Yorkshire, Inglaterra filha de Benet Gernet e Joan. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha com Sir William Dacre. Ela faleceu em 10 de dezembro de 1361, em Eccleston, Lancashire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Joan Gernet родилась в местности лит, еркшир, Англия. Её родителей звали Benet Gernet и Joan. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь от Sir William Dacre. Она умерла 10 декабря 1361 г. в местности экклестон, ланкашир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Joan Gernet nació en Lythe, Yorkshire, Inglaterra como hija de Benet Gernet y Joan. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija con Sir William Dacre. Murió el 10 de diciembre de 1361, en Eccleston, Lancashire, Inglaterra.
